findings.problem: The repaired generic-record contract is internally inconsistent about whether bounds and support facts are optional or always present, so implementers and consumers still do not have one unambiguous wire contract.
findings.evidence: `openspec/changes/establish-bev-element-raster-pipeline/specs/bev-visual-element-evidence/spec.md:4` says generic records carry "optional metric bounds/support facts", but `spec.md:6-15` immediately requires every record to use `bounds` and `support` keys, and `spec.md:28-32` says each record SHALL contain those required keys. The repaired design also presents stable `bounds` and `support` keys at `design.md:63`, so the spec currently mixes optional conceptual facts with mandatory wire fields without defining whether absent facts are omitted, null, or encoded some other way.
findings.recommendation: Clarify the generic-record contract in `bev-visual-element-evidence/spec.md` so it uses one rule consistently: either `bounds` and `support` are always present with defined empty/null encoding, or they are genuinely optional and may be omitted. Keep the same rule referenced by telemetry and steering-media specs.
